title: Worksheet: systematic reviews & meta-analyses
context: Systematic reviews and meta-analyses are at the top of the evidence hierarchy, but a poorly conducted synthesis can be worse than a good individual trial. Patient advocates on grant review panels and advisory boards frequently encounter systematic reviews without knowing how to appraise the synthesis methodology. AMSTAR and AMSTAR-2 are licence-restricted; this worksheet must be authored from first principles.
objective: Author an original, CC-BY-licensed critical-appraisal worksheet for systematic reviews and meta-analyses, covering search methodology, inclusion criteria, heterogeneity, and publication bias — authored from first principles without copying any non-commercial or share-alike checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: Authored from methodological first principles; cites Cochrane Handbook, GRADE, and PRISMA reporting guidance as conceptual authority without reproducing copyrighted checklist text (AMSTAR/AMSTAR-2 not copied or adapted); passes the licence gate clean.

Covers: pre-registration and protocol; search strategy comprehensiveness; inclusion/exclusion criteria and their application; risk-of-bias assessment of primary studies; statistical pooling (meta-analysis) and when it is appropriate; heterogeneity (I-squared, sources, clinical vs statistical heterogeneity); publication bias (funnel plots, Egger's test); sensitivity analyses; GRADE rating of the evidence body; conflict of interest and funding of the review.
Includes a note on when a meta-analysis can be misleading: combining heterogeneous studies, selective reporting, or pooling studies of varying quality.
Written in plain language; questions guide appraisal, not verdicts.
Printable and usable offline; clean Markdown renderable to PDF.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice.
Every methodological concept cited to sources.yaml with compatible allowedUse; passes the licence + provenance build gate and CI.
Signed off by a credentialed oncologist (recorded in the review ledger): clinical framing is correct and nothing reads as treatment guidance.
Signed off by an experienced patient advocate (recorded in the review ledger): worksheet is usable and does not stray into advice.
Signed off by a methodology reviewer: synthesis methodology and meta-analysis questions are accurate.
resources: https://www.equator-network.org/reporting-guidelines/prisma/ (PRISMA — cited as reporting authority, not reproduced)
output: An original systematic-review / meta-analysis critical-appraisal worksheet (content/worksheets/systematic-review-appraisal.md) committed under CC-BY-4.0, provenance-cited, CI-green, and carrying dual high-tier sign-off plus methodology review.
